summary.intendedStageSupportPolicy: runtime promotion requires no expected-label regression, and either the best trajectory match to be one of the expected stage labels or a strong expected-label score >=7 with >=0.5 expected lift and >=0.8 target-video lift
summary.playerMeaning: This pass improved the search process more than the shipped game: no candidate earned enough measured lift to promote safely.
summary.processMeaning: Future challenge tuning can now compare many runtime candidates against Galaga labels before editing game constants.
summary.nextStep: Broaden the candidate strategy to include path-shape constants or richer reference labels before changing runtime stage-19 gameplay.

candidateRetention.policy: Keep the top 120 candidates by selection score plus the baseline row; use candidateCount for the full measured search size.

measurementPolicy.reference: media-backed Galaga challenge labels with comparison vectors
measurementPolicy.promotionRule: Require an expected challenge-label best match, no safety regression, and at least +0.35/10 expected-label lift over baseline before runtime promotion.
measurementPolicy.safety: Reject candidates with enemy shots, enemy attack starts, or ship losses in the challenge window.
